tokens.text: upon them and upon their seed
tokens.range: seed; figuratively, fruit, plant, sowing-time, posterity
a Jehudite (i.e. Judaite or Jew), or descendant of Jehudah (i.e. Judah)
a day (as the warm hours), whether literal (from sunrise to sunset, or from one sunset to the next), or figurative (a space of time defined by an associated term), (often used adverb)
something written, i.e. a writing, record or book

tokens.text: and upon all such as joined
tokens.range: properly, to twine, i.e. (by implication) to unite, to remain; also to borrow (as a form of obligation) or (caus.) to lend

tokens.text: themselves unto them so as it should not fail
tokens.range: to cross over; used very widely of any transition (literal or figurative; transitive, intransitive, intensive, causative); specifically, to cover (in copulation)
to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application
to admit, i.e. take (literally or figuratively)
to rise (in various applications, literal, figurative, intensive and causative)
a year (as a revolution of time)
two; also (as ordinal) twofold
properly, to bore, i.e. (by implication) to wound, to dissolve; figuratively, to profane (a person, place or thing), to break (one's word), to begin (as if by an 'opening wedge'); to play (the flute)
a Jehudite (i.e. Judaite or Jew), or descendant of Jehudah (i.e. Judah)
to grave, by implication, to write (describe, inscribe, prescribe, subscribe)
Mordecai, an Israelite
to do or make, in the broadest sense and widest application
to admit, i.e. take (literally or figuratively)
